quote_of_day.text: Having a sacred animal is the most retarded thing a culture could do. Eating beef is based, it's just a fucking animal. If you think it's a god get your malnutritioned brain checked. >muh milk Every other mammal gives milk, why is cow special.
quote_of_day.name: Ambedkar

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of dietary debates moved over the board, bringing squalls of beef and vegetarianism arguments. Anon traded blows, with one front of criticism targeting 'gobar munchers' and another defending 'chad beef enjoyers'.

A scattered high-pressure ridge of opinions on beauty standards developed, with Ducati_dada's views on NoLust and northie jeetas drawing a cluster of responses.

Nearby, a chaotic air mass of racism discussion formed, marked by shifting winds of opinion and sudden gusts of disagreement.

A brief but intense downpour of frustration occurred as threads degenerated into scuffles, with one anon lamenting the inevitability of arguments after 20 replies.

Meanwhile, a passing drizzle of comments on North Indian women's appearance sparked discussion, with one post remarking on their looks.

A cold snap of criticism also hit Buddhist threads, which have seen a long bad season.

articles.title: Anon debates beef and vegetarianism
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion with a post reading 'Kek, chad beef enjoyers vs virgin gobar munchers.' Anon claimed that South Indians, particularly those from Kerala, do not truly believe in Hinduism and that this is 'really sad' when compared to the state's past during the Travancore Empire.

This poster attributed this supposed phenomenon to the influence of communism. Another anon countered that vegetarianism is associated with Aryan heritage, suggesting that it is a 'pro Aryan thing' and that vegetarian Aryans 'mog' those who eat beef.

A respondent described the preoccupation with who eats beef and who does not as an inferiority complex, seeking validation.

This anon noted that trying to 'mog' others brutally is 'really sad.' Anon also joked about the height and dietary habits of people from different regions, with one claiming that even though Mallus drink a lot of milk, they are still shorter than beef-eating Malayalis.

The conversation turned to the topic of migration, with an anon pointing out that beef-eating individuals often move to vegetarian states for work. Another claimed that much of what is referred to as 'beef' in India is actually buffalo meat.

The discussion was marked by personal attacks and insults, with one anon telling others to 'grow tf up' and another suggesting that they should 'go play ur soy sim in steven universe already.' Anon also made derogatory comments about people from various regions, including Bihar and Kerala, with one joking that Jatts could urinate from a standing position and still hit someone from Kerala on the head.

articles.title: Anon discusses Ducati_dada's views on NoLust and beauty
articles.body:
Anon started a thread mentioning 'Ducati_dada on beauty of northie jeetas'. One respondent claimed that Ducati_dada advocates for strict NoLust, semen retention, and only having sex with a religiously wedded wife for procreation.

This, according to him, is the secret to retaining hairline and youth. Another poster stated that Ducati_dada worships Hashem, the Caanite god of Israelites, and aims to remain pure for him.

Some anons discussed Ducati_dada's old Twitter account, which got banned. One claimed that he used to post about his 'NoLust' philosophy there.

A respondent suggested inviting Ducati_dada to another board, while another considered creating a new account to reach out to him.

Anon also brought up Ducati_dada's analysis of a Bengali aunt's phenotype, which he allegedly posted and then deleted. A respondent joked that this might be why 'northie bros have a strong armpit fetish'.

The conversation also touched on the idea of creating a 'comfy thread' on another board to discuss such topics.

One anon suggested that a good thread on Ducati_dada's philosophy in a different section might attract new users and be more lasting than a discussion on this board.

articles.title: Racism discussion takes a chaotic turn
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion with a simple statement: 'racism is bad.' One immediate response was a massive repetition of 'PAJEET' by another Anon.

A different Anon then critiqued the OP for supposedly being a 'reactionary' who couldn't come up with original ideas. The conversation quickly devolved.

Anon accused another of using a racial slur, leading to a report and a claim of 'sarcasm.' A respondent claimed that physical attractiveness, irrespective of skin color, depends on good facial features. Some Anons resorted to personal attacks.

One called another a 'bloody racist,' while another referred to someone as 'cumskinned fread' after being bodied in an argument.

A respondent joked about having 'golden hair,' and another made a remark about not caring about the looks of 'blue eye blonde hair women.' The tone of the discussion was largely hostile.

Anon questioned the OP's motives, asking, 'why would you post this?' Another Anon simply stated they 'didn't cope about looks.'

articles.title: Threads Degenerate into Scuffles
articles.body:
Anon questioned why threads turn into arguments after 20 replies. One anon noted it's a pattern where discussions start civilized before descending into chaos.

Another anon attributed this to the board's dynamics, citing 'speed, ambiguity, and chain replies' that amplify disagreements. Others pointed out that tone, vague wording, and multiple interpretations also contribute to escalation.

One respondent suggested that lack of moderation and misunderstandings that go uncorrected early on fuel these conflicts. Some joked about their own roles in these discussions.

articles.title: North Indian women's appearance sparks discussion
articles.body:
Anon posted an inquiry about North Indian women's appearance. Another anon replied that North Indian women do look like that and most do not care about their dressing.

Anon claimed that Delhi has a high number of 'bhangalis and LCs' showing a common phenomenon in Chandigarh. One respondent joked about low-caste Shudra and 'dasyu abbos'.

Anon remarked on human anatomy and another joked about moving to Mumbai. Some users rated the appearance and discussed varna.
